Read Runtime First

A good TypeScript review starts by asking what values exist at runtime. Types help, but the JavaScript still runs.

The review move

When a diff adds a type annotation, look for the runtime source of the value and the place where it is trusted. 

type UserPayload = {
  id: string;
  email: string;
};

export function toUser(input: unknown): UserPayload {
  return input as UserPayload;
}

The type assertion silences the compiler, but it does not prove that input has those fields.

Review comment pattern

This narrows the TypeScript type without checking the runtime value. Can we validate the incoming payload before returning UserPayload?

Exercise

Find the line that creates false confidence, then rewrite the smallest safer version.
